ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Monica And Alan Zimmerman'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Nancy Hoffman, Psy.D. (Added To Calendar Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 8/22/23; Ex Parte Papers Deemed To Be The Zimmermans' Motion Papers Per The Order)
Off Calendar Sep-01-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Zabronsky, Esq. of Hartog Baer Zabronsky & Verriere appears in person for Rudolph Koppl;
Douglas MacLellan, Esq. of Law Offices of Kaplan & MacLellan appears in person for

The Court and counsel confer regarding Mr. Kaplan's health issue, Mr. Kaplan's declaration, and the pending trial date. Mr. MacLellan states that Mr. Kaplan is unable to function, addresses Mr. Kaplan's declaration, states that the Handelman and Farley depositions did not go forward, and that they tried to get the letter from UCSF about Mr. Kaplan's health issue to the Court as fast as they could. Mr. MacLellan confirms for the Court that they are not asking to go forward with the schedule. Mr. Zabronsky agreed stating that he is just finding out about this now even though he had said that the parties needed to talk.

Following comments, the Court hears argument on Petitioner Rudolph Koppl's Motion To Enforce Compliance With Deposition Subpoena For The Production Of Business Records, Amd For Protective Order Re Medical Expert Depositions, And For Monetary Sanction.

A brief recess is taken by the Court for the ex parte calendar. During the recess, counsel are ordered to confer regarding how they wish to proceed as to the pending 9/11/23 trial date in Dept. 206.

11:01 a.m. All present in open court, on the record. There being no additional argument on Petitioner Rudolph Koppl's Motion To Enforce Compliance, etc. the motion is GRANTED by the Court. The Court states that it will use features to incorporate the protective order and that it is not inclined to order sanctions. Mr. MacLellan confirms for the Court that he has reviewed the proposed order noting that the trial subpoena language is not appropriate. Discussions/argument are held. The Court states that it will sign the order as framed over Mr. MacLellan's objection.

The Court hears argument on Respondents' Monica And Alan Zimmerman's Motion For Protective Order Re Deposition Of Designated Experts Nancy Hoffman And Jonathan Mueller. At the conclusion of argument, the motion is DENIED. No sanctions are awarded on either motion.

Mr. Zabronsky states that they will be telling the Presiding Judge/Dept. 206 that the trial needs to be continued and that the new trial date won't be before November. The Court notes that it was informed of the following closed trial dates in Dept. 206: 11/6, 11/13, 12/4, 12/26, and 2/5/2024. The Court states that this is an instruction to Dept. 204 and that counsel should consult Dept. 206.

Court to sign the proposed order submitted by counsel.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR #12948, Tel. 415.810.7170, Email: dfuquacsr@gmail.com. Deputy: Bernardo.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Request For Production Of Documents And (2) Request For Admissions; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-09-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Benjamin Kaplan, Esq., on behalf of Monica and Alan Zimmerman in their ostensible capacity as non-party consumers and Monica Zimmerman as Successor Trustee; Andrew Zabronsky, Esq., on behalf of Beneficiary, Rudolf Koppl.

The Court refers counsel to its tentative ruling and notes that Mr. Kaplan has advised the Court that he intends to oppose the tentative ruling. At the Court's invitation, Mr. Zabronsky submits to the tentative ruling.

Mr. Kaplan initially requests an extension of time to respond to outstanding discovery requests due June 13, 2023 and not currently subject to motion before the Court. Mr. Zabronsky consents to the extension of response to June 30, 2023.

In response to today's Motion, Mr. Kaplan notes that neither the current motions nor the Court's tentative ruling prohibits further objection in response to the document production request and requests for admissions now before the Court, including assertion of the attorney-client privilege. Mr. Zabronsky notes the Court has already addressed in the tentative ruling the numerous objections counsel has already made to the referenced discovery requests. The Court advises Mr. Kaplan that it will not revise the tentative ruling to prohibit further objection but cautions Mr. Kaplan from raising objections that have already been made by counsel and upon which the Court has ruled.

Mr. Kaplan commences more substantive and direct response to the Court's tentative ruling, contending that the attorney-client privilege is absolutely inviolate and cannot be contested at any judicial level. He also questions the efficacy of demurrer in addressing deficiencies in the Petition.

Mr. Zabronsky replies, contending that counsel for the trustee failed to raise any of today's oral opposition contentions in its written opposition. Mr. Kaplan responds and submits.
After extensive argument, the Court briefly addresses the contentions then adopts the tentative ruling, granting the motions and the sanctions. Mr. Zabronsky requests the production of a privilege log should a privilege be asserted. The Court will not require the same but again cautions Mr. Kaplan to comply with statutory requirements.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Maloney; Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR# 2737, 415-398-1889, Nogara Reporting, nogarareporting@gmail.com

Rudolph Koppls Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Compliance With Deposition Subpoenas For The Production Of Business Records
Off Calendar Jun-09-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Tuft, Esq., on behalf of third parties Delagnes, Linder & Duey, LLP and Delagnes, Mitchell & Linder, LLP; Douglas MacLellan, Esq., Benjamin Kaplan, Esq., and Leo LaRocca, Esq., on behalf of Monica and Alan Zimmerman in their ostensible capacity as non-party consumers and Monica Zimmerman as Successor Trustee; Andrew Zabronsky, Esq., on behalf of Beneficiary, Rudolf Koppl

The Court refers counsel to its tentative ruling and commences the hearing. Mr. Zabronsky briefly summarizes Petitioner's contentions and submits to the tentative ruling. Mr. Kaplan responds, reciting the history of the case, the status of the parties, the procedural history of the substance of the motion - including an ostensible discovery stipulation - and the California authorities on the attorney-client privilege, concluding that the attorney-client privilege is inviolate in California and that the Successor trustee retains any and all privileges held by the deceased trustee - among other contentions.

Mr. Tuft responds on behalf of the third-party stakeholders, noting certain procedural concerns and the concern that the objections raised by his clients were not addressed in the tentative ruling. He also questions the scope and breadth of the subpoenas. The Court reminds counsel of the limited scope of the Motion before the court and that it does not seek to enforce every request in the subpoenas.

At the Court's invitation, Mr. LaRocca offers further argument.

After extensive argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Sgt. Aguerre; Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR# 2737, 415-398-1889, Nogara Reporting, nogarareporting@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To (1) Special Interrogatories And (2) Form Interrogatories; And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-09-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Tuft, Esq., on behalf of third parties Delagnes, Linder & Duey, LLP and Delagnes, Mitchell & Linder, LLP; Douglas MacLellan, Esq., Benjamin Kaplan, Esq., and Leo LaRocca, Esq., on behalf of Monica and Alan Zimmerman in their ostensible capacity as non-party consumers and Monica Zimmerman as Successor Trustee; Andrew Zabronsky, Esq., on behalf of Beneficiary, Rudolf Koppl

After extensive argument on the Motion to Enforce deposition subpoenas, the Court next addresses the substance of the Motion to Compel regarding the interrogatories, both form and special, noting its tentative ruling.

Mr. Zabronsky submits to the tentative ruling without argument. Mr. MacLellan responds, contending the Motion is not timely in that the various sets of form interrogatories are not substantively different and any motion to compel should have been made at the time of the response to Form Interrogatories, Set 1. Mr. Zabronsky replies, contending that scope of "incident" in Set 2 was limited to one day and one event whereas "incident" as defined in Set 1 encompassed a 40-year period.

After argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling. Mr. MacLellan requests that the tentative ruling be modified to permit a long-scheduled vacation planned for June. After discussion, the Court will require responses to the various interrogatories no later than June 30, 2023.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Sgt. Aguerre; Reporter: Candace Yount, CSR# 2737, 415-398-1889, Nogara Reporting, nogarareporting@gmail.com

Off Calendar Feb-14-2022 Continued To Jun-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Andrew Zabronsky appeared by zoom for Rudolph Koppl (petitioner/beneficiary).
Benjamin Kaplan appeared by zoom for Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman (respondents).

The court asked counsel to confirm whether they have a judicial mediation set for May 26th. Mr. Zabronsky stated that he sent an e-mail to the ADR Clerk requesting that mediation be set in March, but he hasn't heard back yet. Mr. Kaplan stated that he and his client have given highest priority to this matter, that they are ready to proceed on May 26th, that he has been denied basic answers such as who the mediator will be, that he has not been able to get answers from the ADR Dept., that he has received no guidance and expressed frustration. Discussion was held regarding the mediation process of coordinating the mediation Judges schedules with the party's schedules, how impacted the court's schedules are, the pending motions, the need for formal discovery, the matter going back three years, the expense of having an evidentiary hearing, etc. Mr. Kaplan objects to Mr. Zabronsky's request to continue the motions to a date after mediation. The Petition to Determine Existence or Nonexistence of Power, etc., the Petition to Interpret Trust, the case management hearing and status of judicial mediation are continued to June 27, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint case management statement is to be filed by June 17, 2022.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Reporter: Deborah Fuqua, CSR #12948, 415-810-7170, dfuquacsr@gmail.com

At the Court's invitation, Counsel advise on the status of the case with Mr. Zabronsky noting that discovery is pending and that counsel have met and conferred on the same which Mr. Kaplan generally confirms.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky, via ZOOM, for Rudolph Koppl, Petitioner; Benjamin Kaplan, via ZOOM, for Alan and Monica Zimmerman, Objectors.

The court queries counsel regarding the status of this matter and the status of discovery. Mr. Zabronsky reports that the parties have met and conferred on two (2) occasions regarding three (3) issues, for about two (2) hours each time and that further responses are due this coming Wednesday, 10/27/2021. Mr. Kaplan reports of continuing issues with discovery and failure to follow-through since the petition was filed in 2018 and requests a discovery schedule order from the court. The court points to Mr. Zabronsky's status conference statement wherein he states his client's willingness to participate in ADR. Mr. Kaplan reports that mediation was suggested a year ago and that his clients were amenable to participate in mediation at that time but has not heard from opposing counsel regarding settlement discussion since then. Mr. Kaplan reports of concerns regarding mediation being used as a discovery process. Mr. Zabronsky reports that he believes that there is confidentiality assumed during mediation. Both counsel provide additional oral testimony to support their separate positions regarding ADR. The court advises counsel that it will be issuing a Case Management order.

The Petition to Determine, etc., and Petition to Interpret, etc., are continued to Dec-13-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. The parties are instructed to file a joint status conference statement by Dec-03-2021.

Petition To Determine Existence Or Nonexistence Of Power, Duty, Or Right; To Determine To Whom Property Shall Pass Or Be Delivered; And To Pass Upon The Acts Of Frida Koppl, As Then Trustee
Off Calendar Oct-05-2020 Continued To Jan-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Steven H. Bovarnick, Esq., counsel for Rudolph Koppl, beneficiary (appearing by video conference); Benjamin Kaplan, Esq., counsel for Alan and Monica Zimmerman, Beneficiaries (appearing by video conference).

The matter is called at 10:10 a.m. Mr. Kaplan is present but in the absence of Mr. Bovarnick, the Court passes the matter.

The matter is again called at 10:30 a.m. and all counsel are present. The Court notes at the outset that the parties are each requesting a 3-month continuance but for different reasons. Mr. Bovarnick represents that the continuance is needed to facilitate alternative dispute resolution (ADR) and, if unresolved, proceed with discovery. Mr. Kaplan contends that the ADR request is nothing more than a delaying tactic and that discovery responses thus far have been wholly inadequate.

After further discussion, the Court orders the matter continued to January 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. Counsel are to file a joint Status Report with the same conditions as applicable to the Court's March 9, 2020 Order. The Joint Status Report must be filed on or before December 21, 2020.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Petitioner And Or Petitioners Attorneys Of Record For Frivolous Action, Bad Faith, Or Delay By Respondents Monica Zimmerman And Alan Zimmerman
Off Calendar Mar-05-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Douglas C. MacLellan, Esq. for Monica Zimmerman and Alan Zimmerman, Respondents/Beneficiaries/Moving Parties.
Jeffrey H. Lowenthal, Esq. for Rudolph Koppl, Petitioner/Opposing Party.
Steven H. Bovarnick, Esq. for Rudolph Koppl, Petitioner/Opposing Party.

Matter called, argument held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Respondent's motion for sanctions under CCP Sec. 128.5 is denied. There is no showing that Petitioner's counsel met and conferred in bad faith. A party is allowed to amend once as a matter of right after a motion to strike is filed bu before it is heard, if the amended pleading is filed and served no later than the date for filing an opposition to the motion (CCP Sec. 472). Petitioner's request for sanctions to be discussed. Mr. MacLellan is to prepare a form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: John K. Stewart, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Bailiff: Deputy Chan, Court Reporter: Dana DiBasilio of Nogara Reporting, CSR #10118, Tel. 415.398.1889, Email: depos@nogara.com.
